name: Calzone (Filled Turnovers)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups (about 8 oz) shredded fresh mozzarella cheese
1/2 cup (about 2 oz) freshly grated Parmesan cheese
Cornmeal, if using a pizza pan
5 oz Prosciutto or other flavorful ham, thinly sliced
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the mozzarella, ricotta, and 1/4 cup of the Parmesan; reserve.
2. Roll out or stretch the pizza dough into circles. Working with one circle at a time, place the dough on a pizza pan generously sprinkled with cornmeal or on a lightly oiled pizza screen.
3. Brush the dough all over with olive oil, then cover half of the dough circle with a generous portion of the cheese mixture, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges.
4. Sprinkle with the Prosciutto, garlic (if using), salt, and pepper to taste.
5. Fold the uncovered side over the filling and press the edges of the dough together to seal.
6. Brush the dough with olive oil.
7. Transfer to an oven preheated to 500°F (260°C) and bake until the crust is golden brown and puffy, about 15 minutes.
8. Remove from the oven to a plate, lightly brush with olive oil, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, and serve immediately.
Serves 4 to 6 as a main course, 8 to 10 as a starter.
